activities_projects.title: Comparison of Farming Types
activities_projects.objective: Compare different farming systems in India
steps: Create a table comparing primitive subsistence farming, intensive subsistence farming, and commercial farming
Write at least three characteristics of each type
Explain which farming type produces the highest yield and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences between farming systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cropping Season Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze cropping seasons and crop suitability
steps: List the crops grown during Rabi, Kharif, and Zaid seasons
Explain the climatic conditions required for each season
Identify two states where each season is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze how climate affects crop production

activities_projects.title: Major Crop Case Study
steps: Choose one major crop such as rice or wheat
Explain the climatic and soil conditions required for its growth
Describe the main regions where it is grown in India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand geographical conditions for crop growth

activities_projects.title: Plantation Agriculture Report
activities_projects.objective: Study plantation crops and their economic importance
steps: Select one plantation crop such as tea, coffee, or rubber
Write about the climate, soil and labour requirements
Explain its importance in exports and industry
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand plantation agriculture and its economic role

activities_projects.title: Food Crop vs Cash Crop Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between food crops and cash crops
steps: List three food crops and three cash crops grown in India
Explain their uses
Discuss why farmers may prefer growing cash crops

activities_projects.title: Agricultural Map Study
steps: Mark major rice-producing states
Mark major wheat-producing states
Explain why these regions are suitable for these crops

activities_projects.title: Agricultural Products and Industries
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between agriculture and industries
steps: List five industries that depend on agricultural raw materials
Identify the crops used in each industry
Explain how agriculture supports industrial development
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the link between agriculture and industry

activities_projects.title: Green Revolution Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of the Green Revolution
steps: Write about the main technologies introduced in the Green Revolution
Identify two states that benefited most
Explain one advantage and one limitation of this revolution

activities_projects.title: Government Reforms in Agriculture
steps: List three government initiatives for farmers
Explain the purpose of each reform
Discuss how these reforms help improve agricultural productivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about policy support for agriculture

activities_projects.title: Crop Rotation Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of crop rotation
steps: Explain what crop rotation means
Give two examples of crops grown in rotation
Describe how crop rotation improves soil fertility
